name: Old West
description_short: Old West FCU is a member-owned credit union serving Eastern Oregon since 1957, offering checking, savings, loans, and financial services to residents across an eight-county charter area.
description_long:
Old West Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and has operated as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative serving Eastern Oregon for over 65 years. The organization operates multiple branch locations across an eight-county charter area, including Hermiston, Pendleton, La Grande, and Baker City, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional for-profit banks.

Old West FCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products across personal and business banking. On the deposit side, they provide Kasasa Cash checking accounts (currently advertised at 5.12% APY), Kasasa Saver accounts, basic checking, savings accounts, money market accounts, share certificates, and retirement accounts (IRAs and CESA). Their lending portfolio includes personal loans, auto loans, home equity loans and lines of credit, personal lines of credit, and a Visa credit card. For business members, they offer business checking, business loans, agricultural loans, commercial business loans, and small business lending.

Old West FCU distinguishes itself through its cooperative membership model and regional focus on Eastern Oregon communities. They emphasize member benefits including nationwide free ATM access through shared branching networks, competitive rates on deposit products, mobile banking with mobile deposit and bill pay capabilities, and additional services like insurance offerings through TruStage partnerships. The credit union also provides educational resources on fraud prevention and financial wellness, along with scholarship opportunities for high school seniors within their charter area.

As a traditional credit union, Old West FCU is well-suited for consumers seeking community-based banking with competitive rates and member-focused service. However, prospective members must meet eligibility requirements within the eight-county charter area. The organization's appeal is strongest for those prioritizing local banking relationships and cooperative ownership principles over the broader branch networks and digital conveniences of national institutions.
services: Kasasa Cash checking with cash back rewards (5.12% APY advertised)
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Coverdell Education Savings Accounts (CESA)
Personal, auto, and home equity loans
Agricultural and commercial small business loans
Auto and home insurance through TruStage partnerships
pros: Competitive 5.12% APY rate on Kasasa Cash checking accounts as advertised on homepage
Nationwide free ATM access through credit union network partnerships
Mobile banking with mobile deposit and mobile bill pay capabilities
Comprehensive lending options including agricultural loans and small business lending tailored to Eastern Oregon economy
Scholarship program specifically for high school seniors in eight-county charter area
Member-owned cooperative structure with non-profit operational model
Kasasa Protect fraud protection service included with accounts
cons: Membership limited to Eastern Oregon eight-county charter area, excluding broader geographic service
Limited digital-first capabilities compared to national online-only credit unions and fintech competitors
No mention of 24/7 customer service or chat support on website navigation
Business lending focused primarily on small business and agricultural sectors, limiting options for other business types
Smaller institution may have fewer branch locations and reduced service hours compared to major national banks
best_for: Eastern Oregon residents seeking competitive rates on deposit accounts and local banking relationships
Small business owners and agricultural businesses operating within the credit union's eight-county charter area
Consumers prioritizing credit union membership benefits and cooperative ownership over national bank convenience
Local homebuyers and borrowers seeking personalized mortgage and home equity lending from community-based lender
diagnosis: Old West FCU is best for Eastern Oregon residents and regional business owners seeking competitive deposit rates and personalized lending from a member-owned credit union with deep community roots. Primary caveat: membership eligibility is restricted to an eight-county charter area in Eastern Oregon, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this geographic region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
